betterquesting:10.desc:8:
I thought I'd never need to do this, but here I am.
After all the work I have done, all the technology I developed, my infrastructure is starting to become quite a lot; some will say, I am climbing through the Kardashev scale.
I might as well commit. Time to build a Dyson Sphere! Well, a miniature one...
Two main principles will be exploited here.
First, Sednanite; its imaginary gravity is perfect for constraining stellar plasma, or any other fluid mix for that matter, into a small volume that is gravitationally bound.
Second, Defined matter makes for both a great structural material, as well as a transducer within this gravitationally bound environment.
And with that, I can start producing more energy than I will ever need. Right? Right??!?
